methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
ANYbotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $50M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private robotics firms at the same stage and 1 public comparable, reflecting the early-stage, venture-backed nature of the sector.

Two triangulation methods yield materially different results. The stage-progression approach, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $50M raised proxy, implies a valuation of $95M. The peer-median method, grounded in six same-sector, same-stage competitors, yields $1.1B; the peer sample spans $300M (Fairmat, Collaborative Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies), with Bedrock Robotics and Dexterity valued at $1.8B and $1.6B respectively. The wide dispersion reflects marked heterogeneity in robotics company trajectories and market positioning at Series B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $81M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $585M. This wide band reflects tension between the stage-progression method, anchored to capital deployed, and peer comparables, which capture market-based pricing but exhibit substantial variance. Key uncertainties include ANYbotics' relative position within the peer cohort on commercialization progress, intellectual property strength, and addressable market traction—factors that explain the $300M-to-$10B spread among Series B robotics peers.
